free_text: The elastic scattering of He-3 by C-12.
Four silicon surface barrier type detectors
were mounted on the lid at 40 degr. intervals on a
circle of radius 180 mm. All of the counters were
provided with additional collimators in order to
restrict their fields of view to the vicinity of the
target. A single counter mounted on the lower
rotatable plate of the chamber was used as a monitor
in the angular distribution measurements.
The targets were self-supporting carbon films prepared
by vacuum evaporation of spectroscopically pure carbon.
Their thickness was of the order of 20 mu-g/cm2.
*by Authors* A small build-up of carbon on the target
was observed during the measurements. It amounted to a
few mu-g/cm2 per day. In order to correct for this
effect and to ensure reproducibility of the results in
the excitation function, checks were made at a fixed
energy every five runs. Measurements were taken at
50 keV intervals. As a further check on the
reproducibility of the results the points were taken
every 100 keV, as the energy was being raised and the
intermediate points were taken as the energy was
lowered.
